title: mitm_attack
description: An attacker intercepts unencrypted network traffic between a remote employee's laptop and a corporate file server, silently reading and altering data in transit.

timeline.title: Anomalous Traffic Detected
timeline.description: IDS alerts on unexpected ARP activity and unusual routing behaviour for traffic between a remote laptop and the file server.

timeline.title: Data Interception Suspected
timeline.description: Packet analysis reveals plaintext credentials and sensitive documents transiting through an unrecognised intermediate host.

timeline.title: MITM Host Identified
timeline.description: The rogue intermediate host is identified as a compromised network appliance in the remote office. Traffic is being relayed and logged.

timeline.title: Session Terminated
timeline.description: The compromised appliance is isolated. The affected user's session is terminated and credentials invalidated.

timeline.title: Encryption Enforced
timeline.description: TLS enforcement enabled for all file server connections. VPN mandatory for remote access.

timeline.title: Forensic Review Complete
timeline.description: Scope of data intercepted determined. Regulatory notification obligations assessed. Post-incident hardening implemented.

node-1.title: Suspicious Traffic Interception Detected
node-1.situation: Your IDS alerts on anomalous routing for traffic between a remote employee's laptop and the corporate file server. Packet captures show plaintext credentials and document content passing through an unrecognised host at 10.5.10.99.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Terminate the affected session, isolate the unrecognised host, and block the traffic path while preserving packet capture evidence.

options.label: B
options.text: Alert the remote employee to change their password but leave the connection active to continue monitoring.

options.label: C
options.text: Reboot the file server to clear the connection and reassess.

node-2.title: Compromised Appliance Identified
node-2.situation: The intermediate host is a remote-office network appliance that was compromised. You need to determine how it was compromised and what data was intercepted before taking remediation steps.
node-2.question: How do you investigate the compromised appliance?

options.label: A
options.text: Preserve the appliance image for forensics, review access logs to determine compromise timeline and data intercepted, then rebuild from clean firmware.

options.label: B
options.text: Factory reset the appliance immediately to restore normal operations without forensic analysis.

node-3.title: Scope of Data Exposure
node-3.situation: Forensics shows credentials and sensitive HR documents were intercepted over a 6-hour window. The credentials may have been used to access additional systems. You need to contain further damage.
node-3.question: How do you contain the credential compromise?

options.label: A
options.text: Force password resets for all accounts whose credentials transited the compromised path, audit access logs for the stolen credentials, and engage HR about the exposed documents.

options.label: B
options.text: Only reset the credentials for the original remote employee and assume no other accounts were accessed.

node-4.title: Enforce Encryption for All Traffic
node-4.situation: The attack succeeded because file server traffic was unencrypted. You must enforce encryption and mandate VPN for all remote access to prevent future interception attacks.
node-4.question: What controls do you implement?

options.label: A
options.text: Enforce TLS for all file server connections, mandate VPN for remote access, and implement certificate pinning to prevent future MITM attacks.

options.label: B
options.text: Rely on the firewall to block future rogue hosts without encrypting the file server traffic.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The MITM attack was contained. The compromised appliance was forensically analysed and rebuilt. Affected credentials were reset, and TLS encryption plus mandatory VPN now protect all remote file server traffic.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
